titles.en: Chapter on a Man Who Bequeaths a Portion of His Wealth to Another and is Then Killed by Mistake
Chapter on a Man Who Bequeaths a Portion of His Wealth to Another and is Then Killed by Mistake

segments: : قُلْتُ لَهُ رَجُلٌ أَوْصَى لِرَجُلٍ بِوَصِيَّةٍ مِنْ مَالِهِ ثُلُثٍ أَوْ رُبُعٍ فَيُقْتَلُ اَلرَّجُلُ خَطَأً يَعْنِي اَلْمُوصِيَ فَقَالَ "تُجَازُ لِهَذَا اَلْوَصِيَّةُ مِنْ مَالِهِ وَ مِنْ دِيَتِهِ ".
arabic.text: : قُلْتُ لَهُ رَجُلٌ أَوْصَى لِرَجُلٍ بِوَصِيَّةٍ مِنْ مَالِهِ ثُلُثٍ أَوْ رُبُعٍ فَيُقْتَلُ اَلرَّجُلُ خَطَأً يَعْنِي اَلْمُوصِيَ فَقَالَ "تُجَازُ لِهَذَا اَلْوَصِيَّةُ مِنْ مَالِهِ وَ مِنْ دِيَتِهِ ".

segments:
Hadith.5536 - Asim ibn Humayd narrated from Muhammad ibn Qays, who said:
I asked Imam (as) about a man who bequeathed a portion of his wealth - a third or a fourth - to another man. Then, the man (the testator) was mistakenly killed.
Imam (as) said: "The will is to be executed from his wealth and from his blood money (diyyah)."
en.bab-ul-qaim-publications.text:
Hadith.5536 - Asim ibn Humayd narrated from Muhammad ibn Qays, who said:
I asked Imam (as) about a man who bequeathed a portion of his wealth - a third or a fourth - to another man. Then, the man (the testator) was mistakenly killed.
Imam (as) said: "The will is to be executed from his wealth and from his blood money (diyyah)."

narratorChain.raw: رَوَى عَاصِمُ بْنُ حُمَيْدٍ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ قَيْسٍ قَالَ:

titles.en: Chapter on a Man Who Bequeaths a Portion of His Wealth to Another and is Then Killed by Mistake

segments: خَبَرٍ آخَرَ : سُئِلَ أَبُو عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ عَنْ رَجُلٍ أَوْصَى بِثُلُثِ مَالِهِ ثُمَّ قُتِلَ خَطَأً قَالَ "ثُلُثُ دِيَتِهِ دَاخِلٌ فِي وَصِيَّتِهِ".
arabic.text: خَبَرٍ آخَرَ : سُئِلَ أَبُو عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ عَنْ رَجُلٍ أَوْصَى بِثُلُثِ مَالِهِ ثُمَّ قُتِلَ خَطَأً قَالَ "ثُلُثُ دِيَتِهِ دَاخِلٌ فِي وَصِيَّتِهِ".

segments:
Hadith.5537 - In another narration, Abu Abdullah (as), was asked about a man who had bequeathed one-third of his wealth and was then killed by mistake.
Imam (as) said: "One-third of his blood money (diyyah) is included in his bequest."
en.bab-ul-qaim-publications.text:
Hadith.5537 - In another narration, Abu Abdullah (as), was asked about a man who had bequeathed one-third of his wealth and was then killed by mistake.
Imam (as) said: "One-third of his blood money (diyyah) is included in his bequest."
